signatures.label: SELECT column1, column2 FROM table_name WHERE condition

documentation.value: Retrieves rows from a table or view. The SELECT statement is used to select data from one or more tables.
The columns to retrieve. Use * for all columns.
The name of the table or view to query.
Optional WHERE clause to filter rows.

signatures.label: INSERT INTO table_name (column1, column2) VALUES (value1, value2)

documentation.value: Inserts new rows into a table.
The name of the table to insert into.
The columns to insert values into.
The values to insert corresponding to the specified columns.

signatures.label: UPDATE table_name SET column1 = value1 WHERE condition

documentation.value: Modifies existing rows in a table.
The name of the table to update.
The column-value assignments to set.
The WHERE clause to specify which rows to update.
Deletes rows from a table.
The name of the table to delete from.
The WHERE clause to specify which rows to delete.

signatures.label: CREATE TABLE table_name (column1 datatype, column2 datatype, ...)

documentation.value: Creates a new table in the database.
The name of the new table.
Column definition with name and data type (e.g., id INT, name VARCHAR(255)).

signatures.label: ALTER TABLE table_name ADD column_name datatype

documentation.value: Modifies an existing table structure by adding, dropping, or modifying columns.
The name of the table to alter.
The name of the column to add.
The data type of the new column.
Combines rows from two or more tables based on a related column.
The table to join with.
The join condition specifying matching columns.
Returns the number of rows that match a specified condition. COUNT(*) counts all rows; COUNT(column) counts non-NULL values.
A column name or * to count. NULL values are not counted except with COUNT(*).
Returns the total sum of a numeric column.
A numeric column or expression to sum.
Returns the average value of a numeric column.
A numeric column or expression to average.
Returns the maximum value in a set of values.
A column or expression. Works with numeric, string, and date types.
Returns the minimum value in a set of values.
A column or expression. Works with numeric, string, and date types.
Concatenates values from a group into a single string with a configurable separator.
The column or expression whose values will be concatenated.
The separator string between values. Default is comma ','.
Concatenates two or more strings together. Returns NULL if any argument is NULL.
The first string to concatenate.
The second string to concatenate.
Extracts a substring from a string starting at a given position for a given length.
The source string to extract from.
The starting position (1-based index).
The number of characters to extract.
Formats a date value according to a specified format string.
The date or datetime value to format.
The format string (e.g., '%Y-%m-%d', '%H:%i:%s', '%W %M %e, %Y').
